title: HTML Doc Components Sample
subtitle: A gallery document showing every supported component in the html-doc-coauthoring skill.
audience: Anyone who wants to see what visual documentation blocks Agent Kit can generate.
summary: This sample demonstrates the document shell plus every supported block type: markdown, cards, metrics, table, callouts, flow, timeline, charts, steps, and code.

sections.title: Document Shell
sections.intro: Top-level metadata controls the title, subtitle, audience note, badges, summary, navigation, and hero actions.

items.title: Title and subtitle
items.body: Shown in the sidebar and main hero area.

items.title: Audience and summary
items.body: Helps a new reader understand whether the document is for them.

items.title: Badges and actions
items.body: Badges summarize document type. Actions link to important sections or next steps.

rows: Reader profile shown in the sidebar

sections.title: Markdown
sections.intro: Use markdown blocks for concise prose, bullets, numbered lists, headings, inline formatting, and quotes.

blocks.content:
## Markdown block

This paragraph shows **bold text**, *italic text*, and inline `code`. Keep paragraphs short so the generated HTML stays scannable.

- Bullet items are supported
- Inline links can point to anchors like [Charts](#charts)
- Use lists when readers need quick scanning

1. First numbered step
2. Second numbered step
3. Third numbered step

> Blockquotes work well for short notes or memorable guidance.

sections.title: Visual Blocks
sections.intro: Cards, metrics, and tables turn structured facts into quick visual summaries.

items.title: Cards
items.body: Use cards for features, benefits, risks, options, responsibilities, or takeaways.

items.title: Metrics
items.body: Use metrics for memorable numeric facts such as counts, timelines, budgets, or KPIs.

items.title: Tables
items.body: Use tables when readers need exact comparisons or compact reference data.

items.title: Callouts
items.body: Use callouts for decisions, warnings, constraints, or important next actions.

rows: The reader needs exact tabular comparison
The value is not meaningful by itself

sections.title: Callouts
sections.intro: Callouts support four tones for different kinds of emphasis.

blocks.title: Info
blocks.content: Use `info` for neutral context, helpful notes, or reader orientation.

blocks.title: Success
blocks.content: Use `success` for approved decisions, completed milestones, or recommended paths.

blocks.title: Warning
blocks.content: Use `warning` for constraints, trade-offs, or things a reader should verify before acting.

blocks.title: Danger
blocks.content: Use `danger` for high-risk issues such as security concerns, data loss, or irreversible actions.

sections.title: Process Blocks
sections.intro: Flow, timeline, and steps blocks explain motion through time or sequence.

items.title: Collect context
items.body: Gather audience, goal, source material, and constraints.

items.title: Draft structure
items.body: Choose sections and visual blocks that fit the reader journey.

items.title: Generate HTML
items.body: Save compact JSON under `docs/raw/` and run the generator.

items.title: Review
items.body: Open the generated HTML and check that the document is clear.

items.title: Outline
items.body: Agree on the document purpose and audience.

items.title: Draft
items.body: Write concise section content and choose visuals.

items.title: Publish
items.body: Generate HTML and share the output path.

items.title: Write JSON
items.body: Create `docs/raw/<doc-name>.json` with title, summary, sections, and blocks.

items.title: Run generator
items.body: Run `python3 custom/skills/html-doc-coauthoring/scripts/build_html_doc.py docs/raw/<doc-name>.json`.

items.title: Open HTML
items.body: Review `docs/<doc-name>.html` in a browser.

sections.title: Charts
sections.intro: The chart block uses Chart.js. This sample shows all supported chart variants from the skill guide.

sections.title: AWS Infrastructure Example
sections.intro: A realistic sample architecture section using the same visual components for cloud infrastructure documentation.

items.label: Availability zones
items.hint: Public and private subnets per AZ

items.title: User
items.body: Browser or mobile client sends HTTPS traffic to the public edge.

items.title: CloudFront + WAF
items.body: Static assets are cached, and WAF blocks common abusive traffic before it reaches origin.

items.title: ALB
items.body: Application Load Balancer terminates traffic for dynamic API requests and routes to ECS.

items.title: ECS service
items.body: Fargate tasks run the application inside private subnets with autoscaling.

items.title: Data stores
items.body: RDS stores relational data, ElastiCache handles hot keys, and S3 stores durable objects.

items.title: Network
items.body: One VPC with public subnets for ingress and private subnets for compute, cache, and database tiers.

items.title: Security
items.body: Least-privilege task roles, security groups by tier, secrets in AWS Secrets Manager, and WAF at the edge.

items.title: Observability
items.body: Service logs, ALB metrics, RDS alarms, dashboards, and distributed tracing for request latency.

rows: TLS, caching, edge protection, and static delivery.
DNS routing, health checks, and dynamic request distribution.
Container deployment, scaling, and image storage.
Relational storage, cache acceleration, and object storage.
Logs, metrics, traces, alarms, and secret retrieval.

blocks.title: Production guardrail
blocks.content: Keep ECS tasks, RDS, and ElastiCache in private subnets. Only CloudFront and ALB should be internet-facing.

items.title: Provision foundation
items.body: Create VPC, subnets, route tables, security groups, KMS keys, and baseline IAM roles.

items.title: Deploy application
items.body: Push container image to ECR, create ECS task definition, service, target group, and ALB listener rules.

items.title: Attach data services
items.body: Create RDS, ElastiCache, S3 buckets, secrets, and application environment variables.

items.title: Enable operations
items.body: Add CloudWatch dashboards, alarms, log retention, tracing, backup policies, and runbooks.

blocks.content:
Internet
  -> Route 53
  -> CloudFront + WAF
  -> ALB (public subnets)
  -> ECS Fargate services (private subnets)
  -> RDS / ElastiCache / S3

sections.title: Code
sections.intro: Code blocks are for exact commands or snippets that readers may copy.
